mechanism_narrative: A persistently near-zero or declining FEDFUNDS rate lowers the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows and signals continued accommodative policy, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.09 percent as of snapshot
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level/trend and forward 20d returns stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year walk-forward window, or returns following low-rate periods are not significantly higher than the unconditional mean (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises above a 6% threshold, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that precede weaker consumer spending and corporate earnings, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following month.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 10.2 percent as of snapshot
failure_condition: In triggered episodes (UNRATE > 6%), forward 20d SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or the sign accuracy conditional on trigger is not statistically distinguishable from the unconditional base rate (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in PAYEMS and INDPRO indicates broad-based real economic expansion across labor and production, reinforcing earnings growth expectations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter; neither series alone is sufficient because divergence between employment and output signals distortion (e.g., productivity shocks) rather than genuine expansion.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS=139582 thousand, INDPRO=100.1665 as of snapshot
failure_condition: Rank IC of the joint PAYEMS-INDPRO acceleration signal versus forward 60d returns is not higher than the maximum of either single-series IC over any 3-year window, indicating no genuine interaction effect.

mechanism_narrative: Rising HOUST (housing starts) signals strengthening residential investment and forward consumer demand, boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter, but this relationship only holds when FEDFUNDS is low and monetary policy is not restrictive; in a tightening cycle rising housing activity can instead presage overheating and subsequent correction.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST=1186 thousand units as of snapshot
failure_condition: During periods when FEDFUNDS is below 2%, positive HOUST trends fail to precede positive 60d forward returns in more than 55% of instances, or the effect reverses sign in more than one out of three rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month increase in RSAFS (retail sales) signals resilient near-term consumer demand that is quickly priced into equities, producing a short-lived positive S&P 500 return over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: Forward 5d SPX returns following RSAFS surges above the trigger threshold are not significantly different from unconditional 5d returns (t-test p > 0.05) across the sample.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained upward trend in INDPRO reflects expanding industrial capacity utilization and corporate output, supporting earnings growth expectations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC between 3-month INDPRO trend and forward 20d SPX returns falls below 0.03 in more than half of rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL month-over-month annualized inflation exceeds 4 percent, markets price in expectations of imminent monetary tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Forward 5d SPX returns after CPI-triggered episodes are positive in more than 55% of cases, or the average conditional return is not significantly below the unconditional mean (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising UNRATE together with a low or falling FEDFUNDS indicates the central bank is responding to genuine labor market weakness rather than merely reacting to noise, and this dual confirmation signals a deeper economic downturn that produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter, an effect stronger in a monetary easing cycle than either signal alone would predict.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE=10.2 percent and FEDFUNDS=0.09 percent as of snapshot
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle amid labor market stress
failure_condition: The joint UNRATE-rising/FEDFUNDS-falling signal fails to produce a forward 60d return distribution more negative than either single-series signal in more than one of three rolling 3-year out-of-sample windows, indicating no true interaction effect.
